annotations.label: Brouwer's fixed-point theorem

anchor.snippet: mapping a nonempty compact convex set to itself

annotations.note: docs/100.yaml and 1000.yaml list Brouwer's theorem as formalized only in an external Lean 3 repo, with no Mathlib4 declaration.

annotations.label: Brouwer's theorem in the plane

anchor.snippet: Every continuous function from a closed disk to itself has at least one fixed point

annotations.note: No fixed-point theorem for self-maps of a 2-dimensional disk exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Brouwer's theorem in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: Every continuous function from a closed ball of a Euclidean space into itself has a fixed point

annotations.note: No fixed-point theorem for self-maps of a closed Euclidean ball is present in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Brouwer's theorem for a convex compact set

anchor.snippet: Every continuous function from a nonempty convex compact subset K of a Euclidean space to K itself has a fixed point

annotations.note: The convex-compact form of Brouwer's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Schauder fixed point theorem

anchor.snippet: Every continuous function from a nonempty convex compact subset K of a Banach space to K itself has a fixed point

annotations.note: docs/1000.yaml lists Schauder's fixed-point theorem with no associated Mathlib4 declaration.

annotations.label: Endomorphism precondition

anchor.section: The function f as an endomorphism
anchor.snippet: Thus, f is not an endomorphism

annotations.note: This is an informal counterexample illustrating the self-map hypothesis, not a formalizable Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Boundedness precondition

anchor.snippet: As it shifts every point to the right, it cannot have a fixed point

annotations.note: The translation counterexample showing boundedness is necessary is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Closedness precondition

anchor.snippet: there is no point in the domain such that

annotations.note: The counterexample showing closedness of the domain is necessary is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Invariance under homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: Convexity is not strictly necessary for Brouwer's fixed-point theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib4 lacks Brouwer's theorem itself and so has no statement that it transfers across homeomorphisms.

annotations.label: Failure on domains with holes

anchor.snippet: Brouwer's fixed-point theorem does not work for domains with holes

annotations.note: The annulus-rotation counterexample is an informal illustration not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Crumpled paper illustration

anchor.snippet: There will then be at least one point of the crumpled sheet that lies directly above its corresponding point

annotations.note: This is a physical intuition for Brouwer's theorem, not a formal statement in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: You-are-here map illustration

anchor.snippet: which represents that same point in the country

annotations.note: This map illustration of Brouwer's theorem is informal and not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Stirred cocktail illustration

anchor.snippet: some point in the liquid will end up in exactly the same place in the glass

annotations.note: This stirred-liquid intuition for Brouwer's theorem is not a formalized Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: One-dimensional case

anchor.snippet: In one dimension, the result is intuitive and easy to prove

annotations.note: Mathlib proves that a continuous self-map of a closed interval has a fixed point via the intermediate value theorem, the exact 1-D case.

annotations.label: No retraction onto the boundary

anchor.snippet: A continuous map from a closed ball of Euclidean space to its boundary cannot be the identity on the boundary

annotations.note: The no-retraction theorem for a ball onto its boundary sphere is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Borsuk–Ulam theorem

anchor.snippet: has a pair of antipodal points that are mapped to the same point

annotations.note: A grep for Borsuk–Ulam finds no formalization (the Mazur–Ulam matches are an unrelated isometry theorem).

annotations.label: Regular value

anchor.snippet: is non-singular at every point of the preimage of

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no definition of a regular value of a smooth map for the degree-theoretic sense used here.

annotations.label: Degree at a regular value

anchor.snippet: is defined as the sum of the signs of the Jacobian determinant of

annotations.note: The Brouwer mapping degree (signed count of preimages) is not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Homotopy invariance of degree

anchor.snippet: The degree satisfies the property of homotopy invariance

annotations.note: With no mapping degree defined, its homotopy invariance is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.section: A proof using the hairy ball theorem
anchor.snippet: there is no nowhere-vanishing continuous tangent vector field w on S

annotations.note: docs/1000.yaml lists the hairy ball theorem with no Mathlib4 declaration and no formalization is found.

annotations.label: Retraction

anchor.section: A proof using homology or cohomology
anchor.snippet: is a special type of continuous function known as a retraction

annotations.note: Mathlib has only the categorical notion of a retract (i ≫ r = 𝟙), a generalization, with no dedicated topological retraction onto a subspace.

annotations.label: No smooth retraction onto boundary

anchor.snippet: there is no smooth retraction from any non-empty smooth oriented compact manifold M onto its boundary

annotations.note: The no-smooth-retraction-onto-boundary result is not formalized, though a divergence/Stokes theorem exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Sperner's lemma

anchor.snippet: there is an n -dimensional simplex whose vertices are colored with the entire set of n + 1 available colors

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsAntichain.sperner` is Sperner's theorem on antichains, a different result; the simplex-coloring Sperner lemma is absent.

annotations.label: Hex has no draws

anchor.section: A proof using the game Hex
anchor.snippet: no game of Hex can end in a draw; the first player always has a winning strategy

annotations.note: The game of Hex and its no-draw theorem are not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Lefschetz fixed-point theorem

anchor.section: A proof using the Lefschetz fixed-point theorem
anchor.snippet: if a continuous map f from a finite simplicial complex B to itself has only isolated fixed points

annotations.note: The Lefschetz fixed-point theorem is absent (the Lefschetz matches in Mathlib are the model-theoretic Lefschetz principle).

annotations.label: Reverse mathematics strength

anchor.section: A proof in a weak logical system
anchor.snippet: Brouwer's theorem for a square implies the weak Kőnig's lemma

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no reverse-mathematics framework, so this equivalence with weak Kőnig's lemma is not formalized.

annotations.label: Failure in infinite dimensions

anchor.snippet: this map is continuous, has its image in the unit sphere of

annotations.note: Kakutani's fixed-point-free self-map of the infinite-dimensional ball is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Product of chainable continua

anchor.snippet: is a product of finitely many chainable continua

annotations.note: The fixed-point property for finite products of chainable continua is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Chainable continuum

anchor.snippet: compact Hausdorff space of which every open cover has a finite open refinement

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no definition of a chainable continuum.

annotations.label: Kakutani fixed point theorem

anchor.snippet: considers upper hemi-continuous set-valued functions

annotations.note: Kakutani's set-valued fixed-point theorem is absent (Mathlib's Kakutani matches are the Riesz–Markov–Kakutani representation theorem).

annotations.label: Lefschetz theorem for compact spaces

anchor.snippet: applies to (almost) arbitrary compact topological spaces

annotations.note: The generalized Lefschetz fixed-point theorem for compact spaces is not formalized in Mathlib4.
